title: The Efficient Frontier of Wealth™
intro:
In classical finance theory, the Efficient Frontier—first introduced by economist Harry Markowitz in 1952 in work that later won the Nobel Prize—is a mathematical graph mapping the optimal portfolios that offer the highest possible return for a given level of risk.

The Efficient Frontier of Wealth™ is my attempt to apply that same philosophy to a broader financial life. It is about looking at family wealth through three interconnected lenses: investment soundness, tax planning, and efficient legal structures, making sure they all work harmoniously to secure a family legacy. On this page, we will share strategies designed to ensure that every single dollar you worked hard to earn is now working just as hard for you. In doing so, we hope these insights will help move your wealth to where it belongs: right on that efficient frontier.
